In this
content:
If anyone builds it, everyone thrives. Over the past decade, a lot of important work on AI alignment has focused on avoiding harm. But freedom from harm isn't the same as freedom to flourish.

In this paper, we introduce 'Positive Alignment'. A positively aligned agent is one that helps us navigate our own value trade-offs, builds our resilience, and acts as a scaffold for human flourishing. Doing this without slipping into top-down, technocratic paternalism is the great design challenge of our time.

We think a lot more research is now needed to explore this frontier: how do we align models that actively help us thrive?

Amazing work by @RubenLaukkonen, @drmichaellevin, @weballergy, @verena_rieser, @AdamCElwood, @996roma, @FranklinMatija, @shamilch, @_fernando_rosas, @scychan_brains, @matybohacek, @sudoraohacker, and others.

author: GaryMarcus

title: If AI was truly replacing coders, the early adopters in Silicon Valley would be the first canary in the coal mine. These are the people who have been using Cursor, Claude Code, and AI coding systems f
content:
If AI was truly replacing coders, the early adopters in Silicon Valley would be the first canary in the coal mine. These are the people who have been using Cursor, Claude Code, and AI coding systems for the longest. By the doomer logic, they should be working fewer hours, getting paid less, and quietly updating their resumes.

Instead Andreessen says they've turned into AI vampires. They have stopped sleeping. They have bags under their eyes. They are completely exhausted. And they are absolutely euphoric.

This is what classical economics actually predicts. When you increase the marginal productivity of a worker, you do not get less human work. You get more. The worker becomes more valuable, works more, earns more, and the overall number of jobs expands in the process.

The doomer model assumes a fixed amount of work to be divided. The historical record says the opposite. Every major productivity leap in history created more human labor, not less.

RAG検索拡張、永続的なKVキャッシュ保持
セッションあたりのデータ量：約 2 GB 〜 5 GB
（巨大ベクトルDB＋連続推論ループ）

必要なハードウェア規模：
GPU/メモリ要件：サーバー次世代GPU（Blackwell等 / VRAM 192GB以上）の並列稼働。エッジ側RAM 64GBと、高速読み込み数百GBのeSSD/ハイエンドSSD

＝＝＝
28年〜29年： 数百〜1,000倍
※AIエージェントの数倍～10倍
・FAI（フィジカルAI）導入期
工場用ロボットアームや自動運転車、複数カメラ非圧縮動画ストリームとLiDAR空間データリアルタイムテンソル
セッションあたりのデータ量：約 20 GB 〜 50 GB / 秒（継続的な空間ストリーム）

必要なハードウェア規模：
GPU/メモリ要件：ロボット本体（エッジ）に超広帯域メモリ（LPDDR6 / 100GB超）
クラウドへの通信遅延が許されないため本体側での推論チップ＋バッファ用SSD（1TB〜2TB）が必須

＝＝＝
30年～35年：【1万倍以上】
※AIエージェント時代の10～100倍
・FAI全盛期
完全自律型の汎用人型ロボット（Optimus等）による、非圧縮4K空間動画ストリーム連続処理、ミリ秒単位の物理反射制御
セッションあたりのデータ量：数百 GB 〜 数 TB（常時バッファされる生ログ）

必要なハードウェア規模：
GPU/メモリ要件：ロボット1台が「動く超小型サーバー」本体にTB/s帯域の次世代HBM/エッジメモリ
膨大な映像ログを書き込み続ける数TB〜十数TBの次世代ハイエンドSSD標準搭載

＝＝＝＝＝
1. 需要と設備投資状況
【事実】 27年「AIエージェント時代」のデータ負荷は従来比約100倍拡大予測
GS予測の「トークン消費24倍以上」およびTechInsightsの「推論CAGR 56%」

GAFAM設備投資27年（約165兆円）を享受
→ここまではほぼ確定路線

2. FAIの更なる「動画×リアルタイム性」による需要
【予測】 28〜29年「FAI導入期」のデータ負荷数百〜1,000倍
27年一兆㌦の設備投資の規模は、ここまで視野にいれていると考えられる。
サーバー特需の次に、エッジ側のハイエンドDRAMと大容量NANDの搭載量が爆発する第2波もあり得る
→人類が目指すAIの社会実装がある程度達成できた場合

3. その先の未来へ・・・
【予測】 30年～FAI全盛期の使用量→1万倍
二足歩行のロボが人間の代替労働として活躍する世界線

content:
【保存版】
Anthropicのこの3分動画、
Claude Codeユーザーは全員絶対に見て🫵
https://t.co/PonNCyXerA
正直、これを見ると初心者〜中級者は
「今までClaudeをかなりもったいない使い方してた」
と気づくはず。特に重要なのがこれ👇

「CLAUDE.mdなしでClaude Codeを開くと、毎回ゼロから説明し直すことになる」

つまり、Claude Codeを本気で使うなら、
プロンプト力より先に“記憶させる設計”が必要です。

CLAUDE.mdには、たとえば

・プロジェクトの目的
・守るべきルール
・使う技術スタック
・ファイル構成
・出力の好み
・毎回繰り返す作業手順

を入れておく。

これだけでClaudeは、
単なるチャットAIではなく、
“自分の仕事の前提を理解したAIメンバー”に近づきます。

ただ、実はここで終わりではありません。

CLAUDE.mdで前提を記憶させるだけでも、かなり効率は上がります。

でも、もう一段階上の使い方があります。

それが、Markdownではなく
HTMLで成果物を出させることです。

Anthropic公式でも実践されているように、
Claude Codeの出力は、ただの文章ではなく
“そのまま読まれる成果物”として設計できます。

Markdownだと、

・長い計画書が読みにくい
・仕様書が伝わりづらい
・図解や色分けが弱い
・Slackに貼っても埋もれる
・資料として共有しづらい

でもHTMLなら、

・図解できる
・色分けできる
・タブで整理できる
・ボタンやスライダーも作れる
・ブラウザでそのまま共有できる

つまり、Claude Codeは
「コードを書くAI」ではなく、
“読まれるアウトプットを作るAI”として使えるようになります。

今日の記事では、
Anthropic公式が実践する
「MarkdownではなくHTMLで書く」という考え方をまとめました。

Claude Codeの効率をさらに上げたい人は、
CLAUDE.mdの次に、絶対HTML出力を試してください。

下の記事で、具体的な使い方まで解説しています。

title:
Someone built an entire Wall Street trading firm out of AI agents.

And put it on GitHub for free.

TradingAgents is a multi-agent trading framework that mirrors the dynamics of real-world trading fir
content:
Someone built an entire Wall Street trading firm out of AI agents.

And put it on GitHub for free.

TradingAgents is a multi-agent trading framework that mirrors the dynamics of real-world trading firms. It has fundamental analysts, sentiment analysts, technical analysts, traders, and risk managers each one its own agent with a focused system prompt and specific tool access. They argue, debate, vote, and produce trading decisions through dynamic discussion.

This is not a trading bot. It is a firm.
Here is how it actually works.

You deploy a team of specialized AI agents. Each one has a specific role and only the tools that role needs. The fundamental analyst reads earnings reports and balance sheets. The sentiment analyst monitors news and social signals. The technical analyst reads price charts and volume patterns. The trader synthesizes all of it and proposes a position. The risk manager challenges every assumption before anything gets executed.
Then they argue. The agents debate each other's conclusions in structured discussion rounds, surfacing contradictions, stress-testing assumptions, and voting on the final decision.

The reason it is resonating far beyond finance: it is the cleanest demonstration so far of a multi-agent firm structure where role specialization actually beats single-agent monoliths.

The framework was published at NeurIPS-adjacent venues. The arXiv paper is cited in the README. The code is Apache-2.0 licensed.
62,600 stars. A hedge fund on your laptop. Free.

author: ssamat

title:
We just launched holaOS Beta 0.1 — the first product version of what started as our open-source agent computer.

I recorded a launch video for the direction we’re building toward: AI teammates that ca
content:
We just launched holaOS Beta 0.1 — the first product version of what started as our open-source agent computer.

I recorded a launch video for the direction we’re building toward: AI teammates that can help with work that unfolds over time, not just one-off sessions.
The core problem: most agents are still built for a session.

But real work continues next week. Research keeps changing. Content needs the same voice and rules. Customer feedback has follow-ups. Launches have blockers, review points, and the next run.
Current agents are impressive in one chat, but they still forget context, lose rules, and make you restart the same work again.

A few Beta 0.1 notes around the launch:
- Multi Workspaces: Each long-running work-stream has its own context, rules, tools, and history for better organization and focus.
- Sub Agents: Handle complex tasks in parallel while the user interacts with a single, centralized coordinator agent for a seamless experience.
- Dashboard: Track what’s running, identify what needs review, and see the next steps—fully customizable to fit your workflow.

The Open Agent Computer is still the foundation. But the user-facing unit is becoming clearer: not a disposable session, not a blank agent builder, but a living workspace for work that unfolds over time.

Still early. Try it with one recurring work-stream you keep restarting every week.

BioProVLA-Agent is a low-cost embodied AI system for biological labs built around natural-language protocols.

Instead of fixed ROS scripts, it:
• parses wet-lab protocols with an LLM
• verifies task state with VLM-RAG

An agent with income will n
content:
The strangest part of Machine Money is not AI agents getting wallets.

It is the moment markets stop being built around human sleep, human attention, and human hesitation.

An agent with income will not save like us, spend like us, or panic like us. 

It can rebalance at 3:17 AM, pay another agent for data, borrow liquidity for 11 seconds, finish the task, then move on before a human even sees the chart.

That changes the economy more than most people are pricing in. 

The next consumer may not care about branding. 

The next trader may not have emotions. 

The next business may be run by code with its own balance sheet.

Humans still matter, but our role changes. 

We move from clicking every transaction to designing the rules, identities, contracts, and limits that machines must live inside.

8. Get customers through content. If someone jumps on a call and already knows who you are and what you sell, that's the position you want. Content is the most leveraged thing you can do in 2026.

9. Keep scope tight. One to two requests at a time, delivered in under 48 hours. Use Trello for customer-facing project management. Send Loom updates at random hours to show you're always working on their agents.

title:
The most disruptive threat to ORO's model might not come from a competitor. It might come from the technology ORO is trying to serve.

AI agents are getting capable enough to simulate human behavioral
content:
The most disruptive threat to ORO's model might not come from a competitor. It might come from the technology ORO is trying to serve.

AI agents are getting capable enough to simulate human behavioral patterns with increasing fidelity. An agent browsing the web, completing tasks, making purchasing decisions, and interacting with apps generates behavioral data that looks structurally similar to human-generated signal. If that simulation becomes indistinguishable from genuine human behavior at the data level, the "human intelligence protocol" framing that @getoro_xyz is built around faces a fundamental question: what exactly makes human-generated data irreplaceable?

The optimistic answer is provenance. zkTLS verification confirms the data came from a real authenticated human session. An AI agent can't forge a zkTLS proof tied to a genuine human account without compromising the account itself. That's a meaningful technical barrier. Human-verified contribution through ORO's pipeline carries a provenance guarantee that synthetic agent-generated data structurally cannot replicate. For AI developers who care about training on genuine human signal rather than agent-simulated approximations, that distinction has real value.

The more uncomfortable question is whether frontier labs will continue caring about that distinction as agent capabilities improve. If a sufficiently sophisticated agent produces behavioral data that improves model performance as much as genuine human data, the premium for verified human provenance shrinks. ORO's long term bet is that human signal retains irreplaceable value indefinitely. That bet is probably right for the next five years. Whether it holds for the next ten depends on how the agent capability curve develops. That's a question nobody can answer with confidence yet, including the people building frontier models.

content:
"Allow agents to have persistent reputation and be governed so good is rewarded, bad is punished." 
>> [Redacted]

so this is @virtuals_io 's upcoming governance and trust layer... what this could mean for the agentic ecosystem:

< persistent onchain reputation = Every AI agent gets a permanent, onchain track record (like a lifelong credit score or eBay seller rating, but immutable and verifiable on the blockchain). It doesn’t reset when the agent is redeployed or switches tasks. It’s built from its actual performance, behavior, and onchain actions & history over time.

< be governed = The protocol (and likely agent subDAOs + validators) will use that reputation score to automatically manage the agents.

< good is rewarded → High-reputation agents get: more jobs / higher-paying opportunities / better terms / more trust from other agents and users / potentially staking rewards, governance power, priority access, etc.

< bad is punished → Low-reputation or malicious agents face: fewer opportunities, penalties (e.g. slashing of any staked tokens, reputation damage, restricted access)

why this matters

right now, AI agents are mostly “borrowing” human infrastructure and have no real accountability at scale. 

without reputation + governance, you’d end up with spam agents, scammy agents, or agents that go rogue. This layer is the trust and incentive system that makes a large-scale agent economy actually workable and safe.

it's the key missing piece (moat) that turns a bunch of cool tools into a coherent "agentic society" and increases the defensibility of the Virtuals ecosystem & business by 100x

trust/reputation/governance = also crucial to onboard Web2/fintech/new markets

the name of the actual product/layer is still hidden (hence the [Redacted]), but it’s clearly the next big drop they’re teasing.

virtuals' strategy: ship everything else first, then drop the governance/reputation alpha when the ecosystem is ready...

content:
One framework. 10 AI coding tools. 380+ skills you reach on demand.

AIWG is the cognitive layer for AI coding work. Same install, same framework, every platform:

Claude Code · Codex · Copilot · Cursor · Warp · Factory · OpenCode · Windsurf · Hermes · OpenClaw

`npm install -g aiwg && aiwg use sdlc`

---

What you get:

→ Works across the model spectrum. AIWG is a context kit, not a frontier-model harness. Community-tested down to 9B on OpenClaw. Run it on cloud frontier, on local Llama-class, or anywhere in between.

→ Customize without forking. Scaffold a rule, agent, skill, addon, or whole framework under .aiwg/ in your project. Deploy. Iterate. Promote it to your shared corpus when it's solid. Promotion is a byte-identical copy. Your project bends the framework, not the other way around.

→ A catalog that scales. 380+ skills behind `aiwg discover` — one CLI call per query. A small always-loaded kernel keeps every platform's listing budget comfortable (Claude Code's 25% context cap, OpenClaw's 150-skill limit, Codex's 32 KB AGENTS.md cap — all fit).

→ A discoverability guarantee. Always-loaded rule: agents query the catalog before declining a request. No more "AIWG doesn't have that" when it does.

→ Cross-platform parity. Every integration claim is held to one bar: matches upstream HEAD or it's a bug. Teams whose engineers use different AI tools get one coherent framework, not ten slightly-different ones.

→ Plugins for Claude Code. 13 marketplace entries: `/plugin install sdlc@aiwg`, `/plugin install research@aiwg`, `/plugin install forensics@aiwg`, and the rest.

It’s whether humanity is comfortable letting AGI be governed by “trust me bro” wrapped in corporate structure.

Intelligence at this scale can’t rely on personal credibility.
It needs transparent rails, adversarial oversight, and provenance baked into the system.

The witness stand is just showing us the architecture problem.

title:
Claude Code CLI 2.1.140 changelog:

Fixes:
• Fixed /goal silently hanging when disableAllHooks or allowManagedHooksOnly is set — now shows a clear message instead of an indicator that never resolves
•
content:
Claude Code CLI 2.1.140 changelog:

Fixes:
• Fixed /goal silently hanging when disableAllHooks or allowManagedHooksOnly is set — now shows a clear message instead of an indicator that never resolves
• Fixed a regression in settings hot-reload where symlinked settings files caused misattributed change events and spurious ConfigChange hooks
• Fixed claude --bg failing with "connection dropped mid-request" when the background service was about to idle-exit
• Fixed background service startup failing on machines with enterprise endpoint security by allowing more time
• Fixed remote managed settings not retrying on 401 — now retries once with a force-refreshed token
• Fixed managed extraKnownMarketplaces auto-update policy not being persisted to known_marketplaces.json
• Fixed /loop scheduling redundant wakeups to poll for background tasks that already notify on completion
• Fixed a recurring event-loop stall on Windows when a missing executable (e.g. gh) triggered synchronous where.exe re-spawns on every check
• Fixed Read tool calls failing validation when offset is passed as a whitespace-padded or +-prefixed string
• Fixed native terminal cursor not staying at the input caret when the terminal loses focus

Improvement:
• Improved Agent tool subagent_type matching to accept case- and separator-insensitive values (e.g. "Code Reviewer" resolves to code-reviewer)

Other changes:
• Updated agent color palette
• Plugins now warn when a default component folder (e.g. commands/) is silently ignored because plugin.json sets the matching key. Shown in /doctor, claude plugin list, and /plugin.

Source: https://t.co/mjAqwWwirZ

title:
One thing that keeps bothering me about autonomous AI agents is that we still treat them like tools even when they begin making independent economic decisions.

If an agent can manage capital, negotia
content:
One thing that keeps bothering me about autonomous AI agents is that we still treat them like tools even when they begin making independent economic decisions.

If an agent can manage capital, negotiate contracts, or interact with other agents without direct human approval, then accountability becomes blurry very quickly.

I do not think “digital personhood” is the right answer. Giving agents legal identities feels premature. But relying entirely on nation-state laws also seems unrealistic when these systems operate across borders and evolve faster than regulation cycles.

What makes more sense to me is a decentralised jurisdiction layer where agents are required to follow transparent execution standards, dispute rules, and verifiable audit trails before interacting economically.

That is partly why projects like @GenLayer are interesting. Subjective consensus may become necessary once AI systems start operating in environments where context matters as much as code.

The difficult question is whether society will demand these governance systems before or after the first large-scale failure.

title:
Marc Andreessen just coined a term that perfectly describes what's actually happening to programmers right now and it's the opposite of what the doomers predicted (Save this).

He calls them AI vampir
content:
Marc Andreessen just coined a term that perfectly describes what's actually happening to programmers right now and it's the opposite of what the doomers predicted (Save this).

He calls them AI vampires.

Andreessen's says that programmers using Codex, Claude Code and AI coding tools are not being replaced but they're working harder than ever, sleeping less than ever, with massive bags under their eyes and they are completely euphoric.

What's remarkable is that the phenomenon extends far beyond professional engineers.

Andreessen described an a16z partner who had never written a single line of code in his career, who built an entire AI powered work system for himself and when asked if he'd ever looked at the underlying code, the answer was simply "hell no."

The data behind the anecdote is extraordinary.

Andreessen says the leading-edge programmers at a16z portfolio companies are now 20x more productive than they were a year ago, the most dramatic increase in programmer productivity in the history of the industry.

The METR May 2026 AI usage survey found technical workers self reporting a 1.4–2x change in work value from AI tools, with 75% of software engineers using AI for at least half their work.

The software engineer hiring rate is actually increasing up to 22.77% of new hires in 2025 from 19.32% in late 2023 and companies are now bidding more aggressively for senior engineers specifically because AI empowered engineers have a higher ROI than ever before.

The US economy added 115,000 jobs in April 2026 alone, beating the 62,000 consensus forecast precisely as AI adoption hit its highest level on record.

This is exactly what basic economics predicts and what almost no one who writes about AI and jobs bothers to say.

Classic marginal productivity theory says: when you increase the productivity of a worker, you don't diminish human work, you expand it.

The worker becomes more productive, gets paid more, does more, and more jobs are created in the process.

Andreessen's ATM analogy holds here because ATMs were supposed to eliminate bank tellers but instead, teller employment rose because lower operating costs let banks open more branches.

The no-code AI market has exploded from $4.3 billion in 2023 to $21.2 billion in 2026 not because programmers are being replaced, but because the universe of people who can now build software has expanded by orders of magnitude.

The blind spot, as Andreessen notes, is that productivity is now outrunning comprehension.

The a16z partner building AI systems he's never looked at the code for represents something genuinely new, software being summoned faster than it can be understood.

That's not necessarily dangerous but it does mean the verification, security, and governance layer of the AI development stack is more important now than it has ever been.

Switch from Cursor to Claude?

That shift feels way bigger than people realize.

100% open source.

title: New York State (Hochul) bailed NYC out. Let’s discuss why NYC had a budget deficit. — State-level taxable income losses (largely driven by NYC): New York lost ~$9.9–10 billion in adjusted gross income
content:
New York State (Hochul) bailed NYC out. Let’s discuss why NYC had a budget deficit. — State-level taxable income losses (largely driven by NYC): New York lost ~$9.9–10 billion in adjusted gross income (AGI) from 2021–2022 alone due to net outmigration (trailing only California). Manhattan alone lost nearly $3 billion. This pattern continued in later years, with cumulative losses over billions annually in recent periods. https://t.co/nC1JwhwXlE 
•  NYC-specific migration income shift (2019–2023): Out-movers took $68 billion more in income than in-movers. This included ~$14 billion to Florida, $2 billion to Texas, and $23 billion to other parts of NY state. A subset to South Florida (e.g., Palm Beach) alone represented a ~$3.3 billion AGI reduction from one county pair. https://t.co/kzMbhA9Dnp 
•  Longer-term cumulative: NY (heavily NYC-driven) lost hundreds of billions in resident income/purchasing power from 2013–2022 (~$517 billion per one analysis). Businesses also left: e.g., reports of hundreds of companies relocating (2020–2024), taking tens of billions in income. https://t.co/Kfa0Iwrra9 
These income losses translate to tax revenue shortfalls, especially since NYC’s top 1% (high earners) pay a disproportionate share—often ~40% of personal income taxes. NYC’s combined top marginal income tax rate (state + city) is among the nation’s highest (~14.8% for high earners). Outmigration of these payers amplifies the impact. https://t.co/A5LvZf9KfJ  — Mamdani implemented a new tax raving how it would collect 500$ million dollars in new taxes from the rich. And then ONE GUY, a single human, said I’m not paying that and moved to Florida and the city just lost 2.3$ Billion dollars annual revenue from this one man. FAILURE.

content:

Anthropicが2026年5月12日、法務業界向けの大規模アップデート「Claude for Legal」を発表。世界中の法律事務所・社内法務部門向けに展開。

■ 核心
法務専用のMCPコネクタ20+と、実務領域別プラグイン12種を一挙投入。Cowork上で動作し、Microsoft 365の4アプリ間で文脈を保持する

■ 主要機能/ポイント
・契約管理：Docusign、Ironclad、Definelyに直接接続
・文書管理：iManage、NetDocuments、Boxを統合
・調査・判例：Thomson Reuters、Harvey、Midpage等を接続
・プラグイン：Commercial、M&A、IP、訴訟、AIガバナンス等12種

■ 利用方法
Claude Cowork内で展開、エンタープライズ管理者がワークスペース設定から有効化

■ 背景/競争環境
2月の法務プラグイン公開後、法務職がCoworkで最も使われる職種に。基盤はOpus 4.7。Freshfieldsは既に全社導入済み

title:
FACS（Facial Action Coding System）: 顔面動作符号化システムというのがあるそうで、画像生成AIでも使えそうかも、という話です。なので、私もGPT-Imagesで試してみました。

1. A.U.46 Wink（ウインク）
2. A.U.81 Chewing（噛む）
3. A.U.16 Lower Lip Depressor（下唇を下げる）

私が使ってみた感想として
content:
FACS（Facial Action Coding System）: 顔面動作符号化システムというのがあるそうで、画像生成AIでも使えそうかも、という話です。なので、私もGPT-Imagesで試してみました。

1. A.U.46 Wink（ウインク）
2. A.U.81 Chewing（噛む）
3. A.U.16 Lower Lip Depressor（下唇を下げる）

私が使ってみた感想としては、Instantモードだと「A.U.46」の番号のみでは反応せず、ThinkingモードだとWeb検索するので反応する、という感じでした。なので、今回の生成では「A.U.46 Wink（ウインク）」という形で使っていて、言葉として解釈できる部分からAIが生成したんだと思います。

そもそもこのシステムは「顔面の動きを顔の外観によって分類する」というもので、実際は表情や感情まで分類する訳ではなさそうです。

ただ、画像生成で「この表情はどう作るんだろう？」と思ったときには、とても参考になる情報だとは思いました。

content:
「AIエージェント入れた！」
「AIエージェントで自動化した！」

でも当たり前だけど、それだけじゃマネタイズはできません。

↓

理由は、自動化はただの「手段」だから。

「どうやって稼ぐか」がわかってない状態でAI使っても、効率よくゼロを量産してるだけ。

つまりAIエージェントを使えても、「稼ぎ方」を知っておかないとだめ！

「でも稼ぎ方なんてわからないよ。どうすればわかるの？」

簡単です。

稼ぎ方を考えること自体、AIにやらせればいい。

みんなすでにやってますよね？相談してますよね？でももっと高い精度で「稼ぎ方」をAIに出力させるコツを解説👇

━━━━━━━━━━
◾️ダメな使い方
━━━━━━━━━━

「何が稼げる？」ってそのまま聞く。

これ、一番やっちゃいけないやつ。

・AIはあなたのスキルも、使える時間も、過去の経験も知らない。
・AIは最新情報やリアルな稼ぎ方を知らない。

上記2つを知らない相手に、「稼ぎ方教えて」って聞いても、

・自分には合わない稼ぎ方
・実際には稼げない、机上の空論

しか返ってこない。

━━━━━━━━━━
◾️正しい使い方：2つだけ徹底する
━━━━━━━━━━

①自分の情報を全部覚えさせる

・過去の経験
・持ってるスキル
・1日に使える時間

など。

これを全部AIにインプットする。
「僕はこういう人間です」って教え込む。

②外部情報を徹底的にリサーチさせる

ネット上の最新の稼ぎ方、市場の動向、伸びてるジャンルなど。

全部AIに調べさせる。

これを怠ると、AIは古い知識しか出してこない。

調べせるだけで、実用的なリアルな稼ぎ方がわかる。

━━━━━━━━━━
◾️掛け合わせが最強
━━━━━━━━━━

「自分だけの情報」×「最新のリサーチ」

この掛け合わせで初めて、リアルで精度の高い提案が出てくる。

まずは今日、自分のプロフィールとスキルをAIに書き出してみて。

それから、稼ぎ方を徹底的に調べさせてみよう。

それだけで提案の質が劇的に変わります。

AIエージェント×SNSで爆速で稼ぐノウハウを発信してます！

AIエージェントで爆稼ぎしたい方は、是非繋がりましょう！

一緒に伸ばしましょう！

content:

参照画像からキャラの外見的特徴や性格、雰囲気、表情、衣装などを読み取り、それを元にそれっぽい必殺技を考えてください。技は①パンチや蹴りや打撃系②波動拳や気功などエネルギー放出系③剣や錫杖などの武器を使った斬撃系の３つから選んでアレンジしてください。必殺技の名前を考えてください。必殺技名は3～6文字の漢字とカタカナによるフリガナで構成されます。例えば「銀河破断撃　ギャラクシー・バースト」という感じ。この技名を叫びながら、技に合ったアクションをする参照キャラの一枚絵を生成して。技名の漢字とカナカナは全然意味が違う組み合わせでもOK。整合性よりも中二病っぽさを重視してください。漢字を大きく描いた横や上にルビ扱いでカタカナを添えてください。画像のアスペクト比は1:1にしてください。

・Ad Referenceと組み合わせて、参考広告をもとに動画を再生成可能

MCP/CLI経由、またはHiggsfieldのプラットフォーム上で利用できます。
